member.name: Ruth Cadbury
member.synopsis: Ruth Cadbury is the Labour MP for Brentford and Isleworth , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Firearms: Licensing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps her Department is taking to reduce the use of forged shotgun licences in England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data on the number of forged firearms certificates used to purchase shotguns in England is not collected centrally. The law is clear on the sales of shotguns that a purchaser must hold the appropriate firearms certificate and transactions must comply with...

writtenQuestions.heading: Firearms: Licensing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what estimate she has made of the number of forged shotgun licences used to purchase shotguns in England in the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data on the number of forged firearms certificates used to purchase shotguns in England is not collected centrally. The law is clear on the sales of shotguns that a purchaser must hold the appropriate firearms certificate and transactions must comply with...

writtenQuestions.heading: Walking: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, if her department will issue guidance for developers to support walking to school.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 12 June, the Government published the third Cycling and Walking Investment Strategy (CWIS3), which will enable more children to walk, wheel and cycle to school. Active Travel England published its delivery plan on the same day, ‘Worth Every Step’, whic...

writtenQuestions.heading: Walking: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of implementing additional measures across neighbourhoods to support walking to school.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Over the course of the third Cycling and Walking Investment Strategy we will focus on enabling millions more children to walk, wheel and cycle to school by supporting local authorities to provide safe, coherent networks that connect neighbourhoods which w...

writtenQuestions.heading: Criminal Injuries Compensation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what assessment has he made of the adequacy of the length of waiting times for applications to the criminal injuries compensation authority.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Figures up to 31 March 2025 are available in the Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority (CICA) Annual Report and Accounts. The figures for the financial year 2025-26 will be available in the CICA Annual Report and Accounts 2025-26 which is due to be pub...

writtenQuestions.heading: Criminal Injuries Compensation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many applications for compensation were submitted to the criminal injuries compensation authority in a) 2023, b) 2024, c) 2025 and d) 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Figures up to 31 March 2025 are available in the Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority (CICA) Annual Report and Accounts. The figures for the financial year 2025-26 will be available in the CICA Annual Report and Accounts 2025-26 which is due to be pub...

writtenQuestions.heading: Criminal Injuries Compensation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many outstanding applications for compensation are there with the criminal injuries compensation authority as of the 1st June 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Figures up to 31 March 2025 are available in the Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority (CICA) Annual Report and Accounts. The figures for the financial year 2025-26 will be available in the CICA Annual Report and Accounts 2025-26 which is due to be pub...

writtenQuestions.heading: Individual Savings Accounts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, pursuant to the Answer of 24 March 2026 to Question 121139, when does her Department plan to publish its consultation on introducing a new ISA product for first time buyers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that the LISA is not working for everyone. That is why we intend to shortly publish a consultation setting out further information about the design and implementation of a new simpler first time buyer ISA product. Once available ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Large Goods Vehicles: Diesel
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what recent discussions officials in her Department have had with Home Office officials on reducing the level of theft of diesel from HGVs in England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Department for Transport officials routinely engage with Home Office officials on freight crime. This Government is highly aware of the rising frequency of freight crime including of fuel theft and the significant and damaging impact it can have on busine...

writtenQuestions.heading: Leasehold: Greater London
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what data does his Department hold on the number of leasehold properties in London.
writtenQuestions.answerText: My Department publishes a release entitled ‘Leasehold Dwellings’ which includes statistics on the number of leasehold dwellings in England by tenure, dwelling type, and region.Statistics from 2024-25 can be found on gov.uk here. Section 4 shows that in 20...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Leasehold: Hounslow
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, how many leasehold flats are registered with the Land Registry as being located in the London Borough of Hounslow.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer given to Question UIN 127019 on 21 April 2026
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Infected Blood Compensation Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, how many claims registered with the Infected Blood Compensation Authority have been registered by estates of people who died as a result of infected blood.

writtenQuestions.heading: Infected Blood Compensation Scheme

writtenQuestions.heading: British Nationality
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what recent assessment she has made of the adequacy of the existing rights for individuals holding British Overseas Citizen status.
writtenQuestions.answerText: British Overseas Citizenship (BOC) is a form of British nationality defined in the British Nationality Act 1981. Those who hold BOC status are British nationals and are entitled to hold a British passport and to receive consular assistance overseas.BOCs d...

items.text: Name of donor: Federal Department of Foreign Affairs, Switzerland Address of donor: Embassy of Switzerland in the United Kingdom, 16-18 Montagu Place, London W1H 2BQ Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Travel (£380), accommodation (£990), in-country travel (£290) and meals (£240), value £1,900 Destination of visit: Switzerland (Berne and Zurich) Dates of visit: 5 November 2025 to 8 November 2025 Purpose of visit: MP delegation visit with focus on Switzerland’s rail and transport network and Vocational and Professional Education and Training (VPET) system. (Registered 19 November 2025)
